title: tristaniopsis microcarpa subsp. corymbosa
intro: Tristaniopsis microcarpa subsp. corymbosa, also known as the 'Red Gum', is a subspecies of the Tristaniopsis microcarpa tree native to Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits associated with Tristaniopsis microcarpa subsp. corymbosa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the Red Gum tree are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
